cards.text: PM Anwar raised the e-invoice exemption to RM1m on 6 December 2025 — sparing 200,000 SMEs from the digital tax trail.
cards.sub: He cited compliance costs at the Sentuhan Madani Sabah event. The system was originally sold as the lever to narrow Malaysia's shadow economy.

cards.h: Shadow economy is roughly RM330B; annual tax-gap losses cited at over RM70B
cards.s: 21% of GDP on conservative estimates (range 21-25% across methodologies). Tax-gap losses are the explicit rationale LHDN gave for e-invoicing.
cards.d: Sources: Focus Malaysia summary of BNM-cited research; UiTM IR; IJBS Vol 20; LoanStreet citing aggregate analyst tax-gap estimates. The 21%/RM330B is the most conservative widely-used figure. World Economics puts the informal economy at 25.3% of GDP.

cards.h: LHDN: 820.5M e-invoices from 111,600 taxpayers as of 22 Dec 2025; Phase 1-2 compliance over 90%
cards.s: Phase 3 (RM5-25m turnover) reached 80%. The CEO said the mandate is working where it has been enforced.
cards.d: Source: LHDN CEO Datuk Dr Abu Tariq Jamaluddin, reported in The Star and The Vibes on 22 Dec 2025. June 2025 figure was 328M e-invoices — volume more than doubled in six months as Phase 3 came online.

cards.h: One month later, on 5 January 2026, Anwar postponed e-invoicing for RM1-5m firms by another year
cards.s: In November 2025 LHDN exposed roughly RM70m in alleged tax evasion at a single luxury-car cartel firm. In July 2025 5,800 individuals were flagged for RM484m undeclared income.
cards.d: Sources: FMT, The Star, The Edge (5 Jan 2026 announcement). Malay Mail/FMT (Nov 2025 raid). TheVibes (July 2025 LHDN enforcement update). The 5,800 cases involved online operators, commission earners and luxury-vehicle buyers — exactly the cash-economy segments e-invoicing was designed to surface.

cards.h: The question is not affordability. It is which books stay invisible.
cards.text: The question is not whether small firms can afford e-invoicing. It is whose books stay invisible when the rule is relaxed.
E-invoicing's promise was that every transaction leaves a trail. The cost relief is real. So is the policy choice to leave most of the shadow economy unrecorded.

sources: LHDN (implementation timeline; CEO statements 22 Dec 2025), MOF, MDEC, PM Office (PM speeches 6 Dec 2025 and 5 Jan 2026), SAMENTA (statement 7 Dec 2025), The Star, FMT, The Edge, Bernama, Malay Mail, The Borneo Post, TheVibes, Business Today, VATupdate, Comarch, UiTM IR, IJBS, Focus Malaysia, LoanStreet, Zoomotech.
confidence: High — dates, named actors, compliance figures, and PM statements are well-documented across multiple Malaysian outlets quoting government sources. Shadow-economy size is the only figure with methodological variation (21-25% range across sources), and the card uses the conservative end with the range noted in the supporting detail.
